sutta: monks a man enslaves a woman in 8 ways 1 a man enslaves a woman by appearance by laughter by speech by song by tears by attire or dress by garlands from the forest and by touch monks in these 8 ways a man enslaves a woman and being scorned by these are verily called as though in a snare that's the end of the suta
commentary: so these 2 sutas are a bit similar to the first suta the first 2 sutas we heard now in the angutaranika where the buddha said that there is no form that enslaves a man so much as the form of a woman no sound no smell no taste no touch that enslaves a man so much as the form a sound smell taste and touch of a woman and conversely the the verse is also true there is no uh form sounds smell taste and touch them and slaves uh woman so much as the form the sound the smell the taste and touch of a man so as we mentioned earlier the the opposite sex is a great obstacle to the spiritual path and these are the different ways in which this attraction of the opposite sex and attracts a person that is by appearance by laughter by speech universe by sound by song by tears by attire by garlands and by touch and the nexuta is quite an important and interesting suta
